text: The video shows a gastrointestinal stromal tumor located in the proximal jejunum, with visible ulceration on the mucosal surface.
The surgeon makes an elliptical incision around the gastrointestinal stromal tumor in the proximal jejunum and resects the tumor from the surrounding tissue.
The surgeon closes the resection enterotomy transversely, initially using stay sutures of 3-0 Vicryl, followed by a running closure with 3-0 Stratofix barbed suture.
The surgeon ties the final sutures, completing the resection of the gastrointestinal stromal tumor in the proximal jejunum.
